speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Are the knights ready to begin the triumph?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: They are, my liege,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: And stay your coming to present themselves.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Return them we are ready, and our daughter here,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: In honor of whose birth these triumphs are,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Sits here like Beauty’s child, whom Nature gat

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: For men to see and, seeing, wonder at.

speaker: THAISA
text: It pleaseth you, my royal father, to express

speaker: THAISA
text: My commendations great, whose merit’s less.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: It’s fit it should be so, for princes are

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: A model which heaven makes like to itself.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: As jewels lose their glory if neglected,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: So princes their renowns if not respected.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: ’Tis now your honor, daughter, to entertain

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: The labor of each knight in his device.

speaker: THAISA
text: Which to preserve mine honor, I’ll perform.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Who is the first that doth prefer himself?

speaker: THAISA
text: A knight of Sparta, my renownèd father,

speaker: THAISA
text: And the device he bears upon his shield

speaker: THAISA
text: Is a black Ethiop reaching at the sun;

speaker: THAISA
text: The word: Lux tua vita mihi.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: He loves you well that holds his life of you.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Who is the second that presents himself?

speaker: THAISA
text: A prince of Macedon, my royal father,

speaker: THAISA
text: And the device he bears upon his shield

speaker: THAISA
text: Is an armed knight that’s conquered by a lady.

speaker: THAISA
text: The motto thus, in Spanish: Pue per doleera kee per forsa.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: And what’s the third?

speaker: THAISA
text: The third, of Antioch;

speaker: THAISA
text: And his device a wreath of chivalry;

speaker: THAISA
text: The word: Me pompae provexit apex.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: What is the fourth?

speaker: THAISA
text: A burning torch that’s turnèd upside down;

speaker: THAISA
text: The word: Qui me alit me extinguit.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Which shows that beauty hath his power and will,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Which can as well inflame as it can kill.

speaker: THAISA
text: The fifth, an hand environèd with clouds,

speaker: THAISA
text: Holding out gold that’s by the touchstone tried;

speaker: THAISA
text: The motto thus: Sic spectanda fides.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: And what’s the sixth and last, the which the knight himself

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: With such a graceful courtesy delivered?

speaker: THAISA
text: He seems to be a stranger; but his present is

speaker: THAISA
text: A withered branch that’s only green at top,

speaker: THAISA
text: The motto: In hac spe vivo.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: A pretty moral.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: From the dejected state wherein he is,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: He hopes by you his fortunes yet may flourish.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: He had need mean better than his outward show

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Can any way speak in his just commend,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: For by his rusty outside he appears

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: To have practiced more the whipstock than the lance.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: He well may be a stranger, for he comes

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: To an honored triumph strangely furnishèd.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              LORD
text: And on set purpose let his armor rust

speaker:
THIRD
               
              LORD
text: Until this day, to scour it in the dust.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Opinion’s but a fool that makes us scan

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: The outward habit by the inward man.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: But stay, the knights are coming.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: We will withdraw into the gallery.
